# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Kubernetes Deployment for our Node.js microservice. Focus on getting it deployed quickly - we'll optimize resource allocation later based on actual usage metrics. The service handles API requests and connects to a MongoDB database.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1   # Version of Kubernetes resources used in this spec file (apps).  Recommended always set as "apps" or you will get warnings if not rightly defined since it might be overwritten by different API versions. e,g., v1beta2 is the default version now and thus there isn't a need to define explicitly unless we have some specific requirement that needs such behavior (like 'v3alpha').
kind: Deployment   # This specifies this would create kind of "Deployments". There are other types like Services, StatefulSets etc. depending on what the resource represents in K8s Cluster  e，g., services/Statefullsets等。e ,for example if we have multiple replicas and each one should scale independently (maybe for specific workload), then ReplicaSet is better fit here because it ensures that a specified number of pod are available.
metadata:    # This contains the metadata data related to this object like Name, Labels etc which will be required by Kubernetes cluster infrastructure in order not having duplicates and ease management through namespaces (e，g., if we have multiple teams working on these deployments then you can clearly differentiate them).
  name: nodejs-microservice   # This is the 'Name' of deployment. The pods/ReplicaSets should use a unique Name to be able identify which resource each one represents in K8s cluster and so here we are assigning it as "nodeJsmicroservices". In case multiple services share same name, then this can create conflicts on your infrastructure setup ie., Service not found.
spec:   # This contains the details of how to run a pod or replica set e。g.'cpu' 1024m 'memory'-3Gi are required by Kubernetes cluster resources (like CPU and Memory) which can help in optimizing resource allocation for deployment scenarios ie., if we have large-scale/high traffic workloads.
    replicas:   # This specifies the number of pod instances that should be running on our microservice e。g,'2'. It is equivalent to Kubernetes' ReplicaSet and ensure any new instance created by Deployment or StatefulSets can not go beyond 'replicates’ property set.
      nodejs-microservice: 3    # This ensures that there are always exactly three pods running e.,g,'nodeJsmicroservices'. If the count goes more than this, then Kubernetes will attempt to remove any excess instances which can cause high unavailability and downtime of services.
      mongoDB: 1   # The microservice handles API requests therefore it needs a connection with Mongodb e.,g.'mongoDb'. It is similar as ReplicaSet in the K8s infrastructure ie, ensuring that exactly one instance (instance) will be up and running at any time.
    selector:     # This ensures which pod instances are selected to run this microservice by selecting nodes based on a certain label e.,g.'app=nodejs-microservices'. If not specified here then it defaults as 'default' if no such tag is available in the K8s cluster otherwise will give error.
      app: nodejs-microservice  # This specifies that only pod instances which have this exact match for LabelSelector are selected e.,g,'app=nodeJsmicroservices'. If there isn’t a proper selection then it won't select any other Pod (instance). Also, if the label doesn’t exist in node and/or its value is not provided , K8s will try to default back but can fail due as mentioned before.
    template:  # This specifies how container(pod) should be created e.,g.'app=nodeJsmicroservice'   It sets a LabelSelector which ensures only pod instances with this label are selected and running in the NodeJS microservices . If not set here then it will default to 'default'.
      metadata:  # This contains data related to how K8s should create containers e.,g.'app=nodeJsmicroservice'   It sets LabelSelector which ensures only pod instances with this label are selected and running in the NodeJS microservices. If not set here then it will default as 'default'.
        labels:  # This contains metadata about a container that includes its name, image being used (if any), ports exposed to outside world or specific environment variables etc for each of your pods e.,g.'app=nodeJsmicroservice'   If not set here then it will default as 'default'.
          app: nodejs-microservice  # This sets the LabelSelector that ensures only Pod instances with this label are selected and running in NodeJS microservices. if none is provided, K8s uses its own defaults ie., same labels selector which we have set before for template metadata here as well but can be different based on use case or requirement etc
      spec:   # This contains the specification of pod e。g.'image':'nodejs-microservice', 'ports’ : [{'port','containerPort'},... ] are required by Kubernetes cluster resources.  It sets which Docker image should used for creating container in Pod (pod). If not set here then it will default as "NodeJsmicroservices:latest". Also, if there aren't any ports exposed to outside world or specific environment variables etc., no other pod is selected by K8s under this label so won’t affect the port numbers.
        containers:  # This specifies how container(pod) should be created e，g.'app':nodejs-microservice, 'name'：main , are required as part of Docker image being used for creating Pod (container). If not set here then it will default to "NodeJsmicroservices/Main". Also if there aren’t any ports exposed or specific environment variables etc., no other pod is selected by K8s under this label so won't affect the port numbers.
          - name: main  # This specifies that a Docker container with certain 'name'(main) should be created and running e，g.'app':nodejs-microservice, 'containerPort’：3001。 If not set here then it will default as "NodeJsmicroservices/Main".
            image: nodejs_mongodb  # This sets Docker Image to use for creating Container. It has a value like NodeJS micro service's own docker images but can also be another existing or custom-created ones, e，g.'node', 'image':'myNodeJsmicroservice'.
            ports:   # Specifies the list of ports that should be exposed in container to outside world. It will have port number as a key and Docker Port Number which is expected by this micro service for communication within network ie., 3001,27015... etc.. If not set here then it won’t affect the external access of application's APIs or database connection to K8S cluster.
              - containerPort: "3001"   # This specifies Docker port number in Container where our microservice should listen ie., 5972 for requests coming from outside world and it is expected by this service within the network, If not set here then no need to change default.
                name: external-port    # It gives a description of what’s happening when port number above (externalPort) gets exposed as an outgoing communication point ie., "MainService".  This can be useful in understanding how application interact with other parts, but only if it is configured to handle all traffic from specified points.
            env:   # Specifies the list of environment variables that should exist inside Container e，g.'DB_HOST' : 'mongo', others as well like database user name and password can be set here when necessary... etc.. If not defined for a specific container it will use default K8s Configuration.
              - DB_USER: "mongodb"   # This sets the environment variable to point out Mongdb Database Username which is expected by this microservice, if none provided then its going as 'Default', can be different based on requirement or not needed... etc..  It will help in running commands within Pod for accessing database.
                DB_PASSWORD: "password"   # This sets the environment variable to point out Mongodb Database Password which is expected by this microservice, if none provided then its going as 'Default', can be different based on requirement or not needed... etc..  It will help in running commands within Pod for accessing database.
                DB_NAME: "db"   # This sets the environment variable to point out Mongodb Database Name which is expected by this microservice, if none provided then its going as 'Default', can be different based on requirement or not needed... etc..  It will help in running commands within Pod for accessing database.
                NODE_ENV: "development"   # This sets the environment variable to point out nodejs development mode which is expected by this microservice, if none provided then its going as 'Default', can be different based on requirement or not needed... etc..  It will help in running commands within Pod for setting up correct NodeJS environments.
